top_stories.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
top_stories.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities affecting its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, with CVSS scores up to 9.8. The flaws include improper certificate validation in SSO integration that could allow attackers to impersonate any user within the service, alongside other issues enabling arbitrary code execution.

top_stories.title: Vishing attacks on Okta identity systems on the rise
top_stories.summary: Vishing attacks targeting Okta identity systems are increasing, with attackers calling victims or IT help desks to socially engineer MFA resets or weakening. LevelBlue researchers detailed the attack methodology in an April 13 blog post, describing what happens after Okta systems are compromised through these voice-based social engineering techniques.

top_stories.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
top_stories.summary: Threat actors previously using the Tycoon 2FA phishing kit have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing, a technique that bypasses MFA by exploiting legitimate device authorization flows. Attackers trick victims into entering attacker-generated device codes into real authentication portals, granting full account access without capturing passwords.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a major patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

top_stories.title: SailPoint、AWSとの戦略的協業契約を締結　ユニファイド アイデンティティ ガバナンス レイヤーによるAIエージェントの保護を可能に
top_stories.summary: SailPoint announced a strategic collaboration agreement with AWS to enable protection of AI agents through a unified identity governance layer. The partnership focuses on extending SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to secure agentic AI workloads running on AWS infrastructure.

news.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
news.summary: Huntress has identified active exploitation of three Microsoft Defender zero-day vulnerabilities (BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend) that allow threat actors to gain elevated privileges on compromised systems. Two of the three vulnerabilities remain unpatched, with the flaws publicly disclosed by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical security scenarios. It focuses on how threat actors bypass security systems outside conventional IT contexts and how 2FA can serve as an additional barrier in physical-world settings.

news.title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted focused on finding and eliminating orphaned non-human identities (NHIs) such as service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants in enterprise environments. The session addresses the management of automated credentials, citing that unmanaged NHIs were behind 68% of cloud breaches in 2024.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agents, Copilot and Agentforce, that could have allowed external attackers to leak sensitive data. The flaws relate to AI agent behavior rather than core IAM functionality, though they touch on access control boundaries within AI-assisted enterprise tools.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring multi-factor authentication (MFA) to web applications. No additional details about supported platforms, pricing, or technical implementation are available from the provided description.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops outlines five ways a Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on limiting access and enforcing device trust. The article addresses stolen credentials as a leading breach vector and covers privilege escalation prevention and lateral movement blocking within an identity-first Zero Trust framework.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity security capabilities as part of a broader AI infrastructure strategy. The acquisition is aimed at strengthening identity controls for agentic AI environments, positioning Cisco's platform as a control plane for machine and non-human identity management.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is an open-source tool (Rust, Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the Linux host via PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to cryptographically bind tokens to the presenting party, differentiating it from other OIDC-for-SSH approaches.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a new "zero-detection" backdoor specifically targeting cloud credentials across AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba environments. The campaign uses typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communications while harvesting cloud identity credentials.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police have dismantled the W3LL phishing network and detained its alleged developer, who created a toolkit used to steal account credentials and attempt over $20 million in fraud. The W3LL toolkit was a commercially available phishing-as-a-service platform designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of detecting credential-based attacks, which increasingly mimic legitimate user behavior. It outlines detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt to counter attacks that abuse valid identities and authentication mechanisms.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign achieved token theft without deploying malware, operating silently across a massive scale of targeted infrastructure.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites while capturing them. The service bypasses traditional anti-phishing takedowns by loading actual brand websites rather than static copies, effectively defeating MFA protections.
